parent_issue: POL-210 — ANALYZE: Foreman-to-Worker Execution Contract and Connect-Compatible Session Architecture

assessment.summary: POL-210 is a pure spec/doc issue requiring 6 deliverables. The codebase has significant partial implementation of the foreman-worker contract but no formal specifications, no worker acknowledgment protocol, no durable session fields (worker_id, session_id, attachment_capable), and no Connect compatibility groundwork. All 6 deliverables are tractable and clearly scoped.
key_findings: worker.ts executes one child and returns CompactReturn but emits no worker-acknowledged event on packet read.
WorkerLifecycleManager is in-memory only — no durable session record with worker_id.
ChildDispatchRecord has no session_id or attachment_capable fields.
Delegated dispatch sets runtime_state=delegated but does not attempt subagent spawn or write assignment record.
Assignment events (worker-assignment-attempted, worker-assigned, etc.) are type-defined in dispatch-state.ts but never emitted.
status.ts cannot answer 'has worker accepted' or 'can session be attached'.
Connect compatibility is completely absent from the codebase.

implement_parent: POL-211 — IMPLEMENT: Foreman-to-Worker Execution Contract and Connect-Compatible Session Architecture
child_issues: POL-212 — IMPLEMENT: Write Foreman-to-Worker Architecture Spec
